title: Matrix Operations
summary: This page covers MATLAB matrix operations, including built-in functions for generating common matrices such as identity, ones, zeros, and diagonal matrices. It demonstrates how to create row vectors and general matrices using square bracket notation with spaces or commas as element separators. A graphical comparison of two signals f(t) and h(t) is also shown in Figure B.14.

figures.caption: Graphical comparison of f(t) and h(t).
figures.description: A 2D line graph plots amplitude versus t, with the horizontal axis labeled "t" from 0 to 0.2 and the vertical axis labeled "Amplitude" from about -1 to 1. The solid curve labeled f(t) oscillates with larger amplitude, while the dotted curve labeled h(t) follows a similar sinusoidal pattern but with smaller amplitude and slight phase differences. A legend in the upper-right identifies the two functions. Students should notice how the two waveforms compare in peak height, trough depth, and alignment over the same time interval.
